---
title: "digiqual"
subtitle: "Statistical Toolkit for Reliability Assessment in NDT"
---

`digiqual` is a Python library designed for Non-Destructive Evaluation (NDE) engineers. It provides a robust statistical framework for Model-Assisted Probability of Detection (MAPOD) studies, implementing the Generalised $\hat{a}$-versus-$a$ Method to allow reliable assessments even when traditional regression assumptions fail.

## Quick Start Paths

### 1. The Graphical Interface (Recommended)
The fastest way to get value from the package is by running the browser-based DigiQual app. No coding is required!

```bash
uvx -v digiqual
```

### 2. The Python API
If you are building custom workflows or connecting to scripts, add `digiqual` into your python environment.

```bash
uv add digiqual
```

::: {.callout-tip}
If you are completely new, head over to the **[Installation Guide](docs/install.qmd)** for setup details, or jump straight to the **[Launch the App](docs/gui.qmd)** page.
:::

## References

This package implements methods described in:

**Malkiel, N., Croxford, A. J., & Wilcox, P. D. (2025).** A generalized method for the reliability assessment of safety–critical inspection.
Proceedings of the Royal Society A, 481: 20240654. https://doi.org/10.1098/rspa.2024.0654
